input.sub_question: How does agentic AI differ from traditional AI systems?

snippets.text: A tradition is a system of beliefs or behaviors passed down within a group of people or society with symbolic meaning or special significance with origins in the past. A component of cultural expressions and folklore, common examples include holidays or impractical but socially meaningful clothes, but the idea has also been applied to social norms and behaviors such as greetings. Traditions can persist and evolve for thousands of years. The word tradition itself derives from the Latin word tradere literally meaning to transmit, to hand over, to give for safekeeping. While it is reportedly assumed that traditions have an ancient history, many traditions have been invented on purpose, whether it be political or cultural, over short periods of time. Various academic disciplines also use the word in a variety of ways.
snippets.title: Tradition

What ethical considerations arise with the development of agentic AI?

snippets.text: Ethics is the philosophical study of moral phenomena. Also called moral philosophy, it investigates normative questions about what people ought to do or which behavior is morally right. Its main branches include normative ethics, applied ethics, and metaethics.
snippets.title: Ethics

# Research Report on Agentic AI

## Executive Summary

This report explores the definitions, characteristics, and applications of agentic AI, with a focus on ethical implications and implementation challenges. The findings highlight the unique aspects of agentic AI compared to traditional AI systems, as well as the potential benefits and concerns associated with its development.

## What ethical considerations arise with the development of agentic AI?

## What are the current challenges in implementing agentic AI technologies?

## Limitations
